questions.text: Which of the following belongs to the same homologous series as Ethane?
questions.explanation: Ethane is an alkane (CnH2n+2). Propane (C3H8) also follows this general formula, making it part of the same homologous series.

questions.text: The self-linking property of carbon atoms to form long chains is known as:
questions.explanation: Catenation is the unique ability of carbon to form bonds with other atoms of carbon, giving rise to large molecules.

questions.text: The functional group present in carboxylic acids is:
questions.explanation: -COOH is the carboxyl functional group found in organic acids like Acetic acid.

questions.text: Pentane has the molecular formula C5H12. It has:
questions.explanation: In Pentane, there are 4 C-C bonds and 12 C-H bonds, totaling 16 covalent bonds.

questions.text: Which of the following is used to oxidize ethanol to ethanoic acid?
questions.explanation: Alkaline potassium permanganate or acidified potassium dichromate are strong oxidizing agents used in this conversion.
